item.title: Ethiopia Dollar Sales Over $2 Billion as Parallel Market Rate Surges
item.description: Ethiopia’s latest special auction has pushed dollar sales this year above the $2 billion mark, as authorities step up efforts to support the birr and narrow the gap between official and parallel-market exchange rates.

item.title: Apollo-Backed Rackspace Loan Jumps on AMD Data Center Deal
item.description: Rackspace Technology Inc., a cloud-computing services company backed by Apollo Global Management Inc., saw its $1.6 billion loan jump after it signed a data center deal with Advanced Micro Devices Inc., extending a rally in debt that had traded at deeply distressed levels just two months ago.

item.title: US Traders’ Access to Foreign Platforms Draws Scrutiny From CFTC
item.description: The US derivatives regulator is launching a review of foreign trading platforms that let US-based individuals directly access their electronic trading systems, according to an agency official familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple Plans Camera AirPods Alongside Upgraded Foldable iPhone in 2027
item.description: Apple Inc.’s upcoming camera-equipped AirPods, a product meant to vault the company into the AI device market, is scheduled to launch in late 2027 as part of a flurry of new releases.

item.title: JPMorgan Markets Debt for Long Lake’s $6.3 Billion Amex GBT Deal
item.description: JP Morgan Chase & Co. has begun marketing a debt package to help fund Long Lake Management Inc’s. $6.3 billion acquisition of Global Business Travel Group Inc., the travel platform spun out of American Express Co., adding to a recent pickup in leveraged-buyout financings coming to market.

item.title: L3Harris Missile Arm Axyv Picks JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley for IPO
item.description: L3Harris Technologies Inc., one of the largest US defense contractors, has selected banks to lead the initial public offering for Axyv, its missile unit, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: A Billion Dollars of Leveraged SpaceX Bets Hit ETFs in One Day
item.description: Leveraged bets on SpaceX generated more than $1 billion of trading volume on their first day, as investors rushed to amplify wagers on the biggest IPO in history, underscoring the extraordinary retail appetite surrounding Elon Musk’s newest public company.

item.title: Debt Chiefs Tout Benefits of Hedge Fund Growth in Bond Markets
item.description: Some sovereign debt chiefs are warming up to the growing footprint of hedge funds in government bond markets, casting these investors as potentially beneficial participants rather than a source of risk.

item.title: Wall Street Stares Down Crypto-Style Trading All Day, Every Day
item.description: Traditional finance firms are starting to embrace round-the-clock trading for a range of assets in a way that has disrupted schedules in crypto for years.

item.title: Brightline Bondholders Grant Two-Week Extension on Debt Payment
item.description: Brightline Trains Florida LLC, the struggling $6 billion private railroad between Miami and Orlando, struck a deal with holders of $985 million of municipal bonds to extend a debt payment due Monday through the end of the month, according to a securities filing.

item.title: Wells Fargo Boosts S&P 500 Year-End Target as US-Iran Deal Nears
item.description: A combination of easing macroeconomic worries, improving sentiment and interest-rate hike expectations that are priced in will enable US stocks to move higher, according to Wells Fargo Securities LLC.

item.title: Nvidia’s New Bonds Are Trading Actively After $25 Billion Sale
item.description: Initial trading volume has been strong for Nvidia Corp.’s new bonds, though prices have remained near where the $25 billion of debt was sold at.

item.title: Rare-Earth Startup Lines Up $500 Million in Pentagon Funding
item.description: Rare-earth refiner Phoenix Tailings Inc. received a conditional $500 million loan from the Pentagon, the latest White House deal to boost production of rare-earth elements in the US.

item.title: Biofuels Are Key to Preventing Sharp Drop in Farmland, S&P Says
item.description: Expanding biofuel demand is a “powerful catalyst” to restore profitability to American farms, according to a new study, as harvests balloon and lawmakers consider broadening the market for corn-based ethanol.

item.title: Essar Signs $500 Million IRH Deal to Buy Oil, Sell Stanlow Fuels
item.description: Essar Group’s energy unit has tied up with International Resources Holding’s trading arm to source crude for its Stanlow refinery in the UK and market products in a deal worth $500 million.

item.title: US Import Prices Rise Most in Nearly Four Years on War, AI Boom
item.description: US import prices surged in May amid soaring costs of computer equipment, plastics and air travel, the latest evidence of the inflationary impact of the Iran war and the data center boom.

item.title: SpaceX Options Trading Starts Strong as Traders Chase Musk Mania
item.description: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. options were among the most heavily traded among US companies on Tuesday, helping to spur an early 17% gain in the rocket maker in its third day of trading.

item.title: Vanderbilt University to Sell Up to $430 Million of Muni Bonds
item.description: Vanderbilt University is planning to sell up to $430 million of tax-exempt bonds to pay for capital projects.

item.title: Principal Mineral Buys Cerberus-Backed Critical Materials Firm
item.description: Principal Mineral is buying a Cerberus Capital Management LP-backed firm that produces components for printed circuit boards, a key electronic part used in products from cars to planes to AI servers.

item.title: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
item.description: Binance Holdings Ltd. is making preparations to stop providing services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator.

item.title: Ex-Tory Treasurer Merges Advisory Firm Fenchurch With Broadhaven Capital
item.description: Fenchurch Advisory Partners, the London-based advisory boutique focused on the financial services industry, has agreed to merge with Broadhaven Capital Partners to accelerate its expansion into the US market.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Says Inflation Is in the Pipeline Despite Iran Deal
item.description: The European Central Bank must be prepared for inflation that’s been unleashed by the conflict in the Middle East but is yet to be felt, according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

item.title: Race to Turn AI Compute Into a Commodity Spurs New Crypto Boom
item.description: Ethan Vera thought Luxor Technology’s future was helping Bitcoin miners buy and sell mining hardware.

item.title: SpaceX Pounces on $60 Billion Cursor Takeover Days After IPO
item.description: Technically, SpaceX had 30 days following its record-shattering public debut to decide on a $60 billion takeover of the AI coding startup Cursor. In the end, all it took was two trading days.

item.title: World Bank Initiative Brings Electricity to 50 Million Africans
item.description: A World Bank initiative to provide electricity to 300 million Africans by 2030 has reached nearly a fifth of its goal, two years after its formation.

item.title: CSN Cement Sale Faces Questions as Holcim Resists Huaxin Bid
item.description: Huaxin Cement Co.’s bid for the cement unit of Brazil’s Cia. Siderurgia Nacional is facing resistance from one of the Chinese company’s key shareholders, Holcim Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: UzNIF to Start Preparing IPO of Uzbekistan Airways
item.description: Uzbekistan’s National Investment Fund, or UzNIF, will start preparations for an initial public offering of Uzbekistan Airways as the fund moves to the next stage of its capital-attraction strategy.

item.title: Former De Beers CEO Penny Is Said to Lead Race for Diamond Giant
item.description: A consortium led by De Beers’s former boss Gareth Penny is leading the race to buy the world’s biggest diamond miner after the Iran war hampered other bidders, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: The Executive Coach Showing Sam Altman How to Lead With ‘Emotional Fluidity’
item.description: Joe Hudson is sure the winners of the impending tech revolution will be human.

item.title: Portobello Delays Sale of Legal-Tech Firm on AI Disruption Angst
item.description: Private equity firm Portobello Capital is delaying its sale of Spanish legal-tech firm Legálitas due to concerns by prospective investors that artificial intelligence will disrupt the sector, according to people familiar with the matter.
